categories.title: WHAT'S IN A NAME?
categories.theme: Food and drink items whose names have a literal meaning in another language — answer is the FOOD or DRINK item

clues.clue: This word for a beloved Italian pasta literally means 'little twine' in its home language.
The Yiddish word for 'ring' or 'bracelet' gives us the name of this breakfast staple, often topped with cream cheese.
In Italian, this tube-shaped pasta's name translates to 'small thimbles.'
This Mexican dish's name is Spanish for 'little donkey,' perhaps owing to the stuffed, rolled shape.
In Russian, this fermented grain beverage's name literally means 'little water,' echoing vodka's humbler cousin.

categories.title: PASSPORT, PLEASE
categories.theme: Dishes and drinks strongly associated with a specific country or region — answer is the COUNTRY or REGION

clues.clue: Pack your chopsticks — Ramen, that hearty noodle soup, is most closely associated with this island nation.
Poutine — fries topped with cheese curds and gravy — is the beloved comfort food of this North American country.
Stamppot, a hearty mash of potatoes and vegetables, is a traditional dish of this Western European nation known for tulips and windmills.
Gallo Pinto, a dish of rice and beans, is the national staple of this Central American country.
Plov, a fragrant rice dish cooked with lamb and carrots, is most closely associated with this Central Asian republic along the Silk Road.

finals.title: A LAYERED LEGACY
finals.clue: This British cheese, created by layering two distinct varieties, pairs creamy Double Gloucester with the pungent blue veins of Stilton.

finals.title: HOTEL BAR HISTORY
finals.clue: First mixed at a legendary colonial-era hotel in Singapore, this gin-based cocktail shares its name with the city-state where it was born.
